cards.name: ACHILLES

cards.name: AENEAS

abilities.name: Gorgi Epithesi
abilities.text: Once each round, if you are adjacent to an opposing warrior, move one space. After the move, if you are no longer adjacent to that warrior, deal 1 damage to them.

cards.flavor: Aeneas was said to be the son of the goddess Aphrodite. He was the leader of the Dardans, allies of Troy, and is the legendary founder of Rome.

abilities.text: While you have a rival, gain +1 speed and +5 experience.

abilities.name: ‘fd

cards.flavor: Born into a crumbling Egypt slowly losing ground to the expanding Roman Empire, Cleopatra used every tool at her disposal to maintain independence and return her nation to the glory that it once held.
cards.raw_source_text: CLEOPATRA VII (Aether) 6 Life 3 Speed +2 8 Experience � 1 Damage +2 Egyptian • Warrior • Female ‘fd: At the start of each round, if there is an opposing male warrior, you may move one space. cards.name: CÚ CHULAINN

abilities.name: Fíoch Fola
abilities.text: At the start of each of your turns, if any opponent revealed a support card that round with initiative equal to or higher than your life, you may move them to any space in your basic attack grid. If you do, you cannot move more than once during that turn.

cards.flavor: “It is a wonderful thing if I am but one day and one night in the world, provided that my fame and my deeds live after me.” — Cú Chulainn

cards.name: CUITLÁHUAC

abilities.name: Amana
abilities.text: Once each round, after you hit with a basic attack, you may immediately make an attack with one of your weapons, if possible. This attack cannot deal more than the weapon’s base damage.

cards.flavor: Succeeding his brother Moctezuma II, Cuitláhuac led a major Aztec victory against the Spanish, in which 400 conquistadors and thousands of their Mesoamerican allies were slain.

cards.name: CYRUS THE GREAT

abilities.name: Savarkar-e Chabok
abilities.text: While you have a cavalry card, you gain +1 speed.

cards.flavor: The greatest king of the Achaemenid dynasty, Cyrus the Great conquered mighty Babylon, expanded the Persian Empire on all fronts, and is credited with creating the first charter of human rights.

cards.name: D’ARTAGNAN

abilities.name: Riposte
abilities.text: Once each turn, after you make an attack or defense roll, if the attack misses, you may move one space. Your next attack roll with a French sword gains +1.

cards.flavor: Although immortalized in Dumas' novel The Three Musketeers, the real D'Artagnan was a musketeer proper, and rose to the position of Commander of the King's Grand Musketeers, the most coveted military appointment in France.

cards.name: DANIEL BOONE

abilities.name: Improvise
abilities.text: When you make a basic attack, you may choose to have it count as a polearm weapon attack instead.

abilities.name: Wily
abilities.text: Once each round, you may re-roll one die of one of your defense rolls. If the new result matches the old result, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: “I cannot say as ever I was lost, but I was bewildered once for three days.” – Daniel Boone

cards.name: DARIUS THE GREAT

abilities.name: Ghahremani
abilities.text: At the beginning of each round, roll two dice. If this total is even, your attack rolls gain +1 for the rest of the game. If this total is odd, your defense rolls gain +1 this round. If you roll doubles, your next attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: After putting down revolts in Persia, Media, Babylon, and the East, Darius the Great set about reforming his Empire. He later led campaigns extending into Macedonia, Thrace, and, according to Herodotus, even as far as the river Volga.

abilities.text: Whenever an opponent reveals a card from a different culture than their warrior, they get –2 experience.

cards.flavor: Fortis, ferox et celer.

cards.name: EL CID

abilities.name: Maniobra Estrategica
abilities.text: Once each round, before you make an attack or use an action ability, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: One of the leading generals in the Reconquista, El Cid became a mercenary for both sides after the jealous King Alfonso VI exiled him. El Cid later captured the city of Valencia, ruling it until his death in 1099.

abilities.name: Maestro de Armas
abilities.text: Your attack and defense rolls gain +2 against an opponent with a weapon of the same type as your weapon.

cards.flavor: Finn MacCumhaill was the leader of the Fianna, whoe reputedly defeated early Roman landings on Irish shores so savagely that the Romans never returned in numbers.

abilities.name: Betrügerischer Plan
abilities.text: You may make two attacks each turn with each of your knives. Your attack rolls gain +1 while you are directly or diagonally behind the defender.

cards.flavor: Rising from a lowly palace servant to become the Queen of Soissons, Fredegunde employed intrigue, manipulation, and assassination in order to secure her kingdom for herself and her son. In a murderous age, she proved that the shadows were the most dangerous weapon of all.

cards.flavor: Glass fought and killed a she-grizzly, and then crawled 200 miles to civilization despite horrific wounds.

abilities.text: Players cannot use abilities on weapon cards. At the start of each round, if you did not win initiative and have less life than all opponents, this ability affects only your opponents that round.

cards.name: JAMES BECKWOURTH

cards.name: THEODERICH DER GROSSE

abilities.text: Once per round, after an attack misses you, you may immediately make an attack, if possible. If you do, you get –1 speed on your next turn. This attack deals +1 damage.

abilities.name: Prosarmogi
abilities.text: At the start of each round, choose one of your card abilities that gives you a defense roll bonus. Your first attack roll of that round gains a bonus equal to the chosen defense roll bonus.

abilities.text: When you hit with a basic attack, the defender chooses to either get –1 to all of their rolls on their next turn or for you to gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: If the attack was a critical hit, apply both options. Inexorable and cruel, Tlacaelel elevated the bloodthirsty cult of Huitzilopochtli by dedicating the seventh reconstruction of the Templo Mayor to him.

abilities.text: Defense rolls cannot be modified. Once per round when you hit, if the defender has no shield or Armor cards in play, your attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Vercingétorix ably unified the Gallic tribes, adopted the policy of retreating to natural fortifications, and undertook an early example of a scorched-earth strategy by burning towns to prevent the Roman legions from living off the land.

abilities.name: Han dong shan feng
abilities.text: You gain +1 speed for each of your face-up support cards.

abilities.text: If you are a male warrior, gain +1 speed and get –3 experience.

abilities.name: Thanaa

cards.name: ATHINA

abilities.name: Evlogimeni Stratigiki
abilities.text: At the end of each opponent’s turn, if you won initiative that round, you may take one action. If you attack with this action, the attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: Grendel the giant will dine with anyone who enters "his" hall after dark.

cards.name: GUAN DI

abilities.name: Zhan shi zhi guang
abilities.text: Choose either attack or defense. For this round, your rolls of that type gain +2 and your opponent’s rolls of that type gain +1.

cards.flavor: Patron god of the Chinese martial arts, Guan Di symbolizes justice, loyalty, righteousness and humility, as well as military and fighting prowess.

abilities.text: Before initiative is determined each round, roll two dice. cards.flavor: — Tariq ibn Ziyad, recounted by al-Maqqari.

cards.name: HEIMDAL

abilities.name: Wachsamkeit
abilities.text: You cannot be damaged by any opponent outside of that opponent’s turn.

cards.flavor: As the watchman of the gods, Heimdal can see one hundred leagues in every direction and can even hear grass grow. He is the god of light, and guards Bifrost, the only entrance to Asgard. At the final conflict of Ragnarok he will kill his age-old enemy, the evil god Loki.

abilities.text: Once per roll, after dice are rolled, you may deal one damage to your warrior. If you do, force any one die to be re-rolled.

cards.flavor: Son of Ast (or Isis) and Osiris, Horus fought against his uncle Seth for control of Egypt. Eventually, he won and exiled Seth to the Sahara Desert, but not before his eye was destroyed by Seth.

abilities.text: Once per round, after an attack or defense roll, you may increase one of the dice you rolled by 1 and decrease one of the dice your opponent rolled by 1.

cards.flavor: One of the techniques used to achieve “illumination,” the Iombhas Forasna was used for poetic inspiration and divination, and a Druid or a Filidh could be expected to know it after passing through the eighth year of study.

abilities.text: When you attack, deal 1 damage to the defender for each die that is a 6 in the defense roll.

cards.flavor: “Men of this country, being murderers, impute their sordid practice to divine command. That any god is evil I do not believe.” – Euripides, Iphigenia in Tauris, 390

cards.name: JIHAD

abilities.name: Radabilej
abilities.text: If all of your cards are Saracen, your rolls gain +1.

cards.flavor: The word “jihad” derives from the Arabic root “jhd,” meaning “to strive to the utmost”. In the Sufi tradition, Jihad al-Akbar is the greater jihad, and is the spiritual struggle against one’s inner self (nafs) and desires (hawa). Jihad al-Asgar is the lesser jihad. It is external and refers to physical effort, including war.

abilities.name: Telchihua
abilities.text: After an opponent makes a roll, if the roll is less than that player’s initiative for the round, you may discard this card. If you do, that player must discard one of their non-weapon support cards.

cards.flavor: The god of beauty, might and war, his smoking mirror killed all who inhaled its fumes. The twin of Quetzalcoatl, together they captured the great monster Cipatli and created the lands from her body.

cards.name: THETIS

abilities.name: Mitriki Agapi
abilities.text: Once per game, re-roll any one die or make your opponent re-roll any one die.

cards.flavor: Daughter of Nereus and Doris, Thetis is the queen of the sea nymphs and mother of Achilles.

cards.name: THIGMENI TIMI

abilities.name: Prodomenos
abilities.text: At the start of each round, choose an opponent that revealed a card from a culture different than that of their warrior. That warrior is your rival for this round.

abilities.name: Pairno Ekdikisi
abilities.text: After one of your rivals rolls doubles, discard this card.

cards.flavor: Betrayed by his royal Trojan guests, Meneláos’s aggrieved honor demanded reparation, and so the Trojan War was planned and launched.

cards.name: THOR

abilities.name: Torden
abilities.text: After you roll doubles on a defense roll, deal 1 damage to the attacking warrior.

cards.flavor: Thor, the Norse god of thunder, protects human and god alike from the forces of evil.

cards.name: TIWAZ

abilities.name: Freier Himmel
abilities.text: After any player makes a critical hit, you may discard this card. If you do, the hit damages the attacker instead of the defender.

cards.flavor: God of war and the patron god of justice, Tiwaz is the oldest of the gods, inspiring courage and heroism in battle. He sacrificed his right hand to bind the wolf Fenrir, who, if set free, would devour the sun. At the day of Ragnarok, he will die from his battle wounds, but not before he slays the hound Garm, the guardian of hell.
cards.raw_source_text: (9) TIWAZ (Earth) Germanic • Inspiration • Deity Freier Himmel: After any player makes a critical hit, you may discard this card. cards.name: TLALOC

abilities.name: Tlacelmilhuiliztli
abilities.text: At the end of this round, if you took more damage from opponents than you dealt, your attacks deal +1 damage for the rest of the game.

cards.flavor: Rain god and ruler over the third of five worlds, Tlaloc was greatly feared as the bearer of drought and flood, despite his associations with fertility and life.

abilities.text: Choose another warrior. Exchange the experience values of your warrior and the chosen warrior.

abilities.text: For this round, weapon attacks made against you deal –2 damage, to a minimum of 0.

cards.flavor: The Grand Master of Santiago, Alonso de Cárdenas, praised the splendor of Córdoba’s armor, saying he was conspicuous on the field due both to his bravery and the armor’s magnificence.
cards.raw_source_text: (2) ARMADURA COMPLETA Spanish • Armor • Torso Irrompible – Reveal: For this round, weapon attacks made against you deal –2 damage, to a minimum of 0. Illustration: Drew Baker 5 89/100

cards.name: ARMOR OF HEPHAESTUS

abilities.name: Eulogimeni Panoplia
abilities.text: Once per round, when you attack, you may make your enemy re-roll their defense roll. Discard this card if the result is lower than their original roll.

cards.flavor: After Hector takes Achilles’ armor, Hephaestus forges a replacement panoply like nothing ever seen.

Illustration: Chris Appel 1 94/100

cards.name: ARTICLES OF INTERROGATORY

abilities.name: Taispeáin Meon
abilities.text: If the total initiative of your cards is less than 18, your rolls gain +1. At the start of any round, if the total initiative of your cards is 18, discard this card and gain +2 speed for that round.

cards.flavor: Queen Elizabeth I sent 18 questions to Gráinne. Her answers guaranteed her safe passage, where otherwise she might have been arrested and summarily executed.

cards.name: BARGUSTUWAN

abilities.name: Derra Elhosan
abilities.text: Your cavalry cards cannot be discarded by an opponent’s card abilities.

abilities.name: Amr Belmotarada
abilities.text: Once each round, after an opponent uses an action ability, or moves out of a space in your warrior’s basic attack grid, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: A simple, quilted covering, the bargustuwan provided protection for a mount while remaining sufficiently light as to leave the horse’s maneuverability unhindered.

cards.name: BEAR HIDE

abilities.name: Hard-fought
abilities.text: After the next time you are hit, your defense rolls gain +2 until this card leaves play or this ability is used again.

cards.flavor: Left for dead, and with only a bearskin to protect himself from the elements, Glass set his own broken leg and set out on a legendary trek for survival.

cards.name: BEAR SARK

abilities.name: Bjorn
abilities.text: Once per round, when you are attacked, force the attacker to re-roll their attack roll. Discard this card if the result is lower than the original roll.

cards.flavor: Wearing this pelt gives the Norse warrior the strength, stamina and power of the bear itself.

cards.name: BLANC HARNOIS

abilities.name: Éclat Immaculé
abilities.text: Your attacks deal +1 damage to a player who has used a Reveal ability this game.

cards.flavor: After liberating Orleans, Jargeau, Meung-sur-Loire, Beaugency, and Patay from the English, Joan of Arc marched in her brilliant white armor to Rheims to see Charles crowned king.

cards.name: BO XIONG JIA

abilities.name: Zu dang
abilities.text: If you are attacked and missed, the attacking opponent cannot attack you with their next action this turn.

cards.flavor: Lamellar armor is made by binding together metal scales into a small plate, which is then covered with lacquer to make it waterproof. These small, light plates are fastened together with cords of leather, each plate slightly overlapping the other.
cards.raw_source_text: (3) BO XIONG JIA Chinese • Armor • Torso Zu dang: If you are attacked and missed, the attacking opponent cannot attack you with their next action this turn. cards.name: BOONESBOROUGH

abilities.name: Sharpened Pikes
abilities.text: When you attack with a polearm, you may re-roll one die of the attack or defense roll.

abilities.name: Secluded
abilities.text: After an opposing warrior moves into a space adjacent to you, your next defense roll gains +2.

cards.flavor: Boonesborough was a fortified village, built in 1775 in Kentucky. Boone moved his family over the Wilderness Trail to live there.
cards.raw_source_text: (2) BOONESBOROUGH American Frontiersmen • Armor • Location Sharpened Pikes: When you attack with a polearm, you may re-roll one die of the attack or defense roll. cards.name: BOUCLIER CAROLINGIEN

abilities.name: Prêt à Servir
abilities.text: While an opponent is in your basic attack grid, your defense and basic attack rolls gain +1. You may use another Armor.

cards.flavor: Because of their skill with disposable wood construction, Charlemagne ordered shield-makers stationed in every district and specifically brought them along on campaigns.

cards.name: BOUCLIER CELTIQUE

abilities.name: Coup de Bouclier
abilities.text: Once per round, when you hit an opponent, if the highest die of the attack roll is greater than the highest die roll of the defense roll, the attack deals +1 damage and you may move the defender one space or rotate them.

cards.flavor: Meeting with little organized resistance, Caesar moved his armies through Gaul using a careful divide-and-conquer strategy. Vercingetorix successfully united many of the independent tribes to fight against the invading conqueror.
cards.raw_source_text: (6) BOUCLIER CELTIQUE French • Armor • Shield (1 Hand) Coup de Bouclier: Once per round, when you hit an opponent, if the highest die of the attack roll is greater than the highest die roll of the defense roll, the attack deals +1 damage and you may move the defender one space or rotate them. cards.name: BRAT DERG

abilities.name: Seasamh Uasal
abilities.text: Attacks against you cannot gain damage bonuses if the attacker has fewer cards that match their warrior’s culture than you have that match yours.

cards.flavor: Medb accentuated her beauty with the richest of colors and fabrics. Never were men reticent in trying to win her favor.

cards.name: BRATT

abilities.name: Tangilen
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +2 against polearm and ranged weapons.

cards.flavor: A coarse woolen cloak, the bratt was an indispensable garment to any northern Celt. Large enough to double as a blanket when sleeping outdoors, it could be worn long and sweeping for warmth and could be folded and draped for ease of movement.

cards.name: BROIGNE D’ÉCAILLES

abilities.name: Posture Défensive
abilities.text: Once per turn, after you make a defense roll, you may have the roll gain +3. If you do, you get –1 speed for your next turn.

cards.flavor: Only once in the history of armed combat has an army of infantry without bows and arrows or firearms withstood armored cavalry. This honor goes to the hauberk-armored men of Charles Martel and his brilliant defensive tactics.
cards.raw_source_text: (5) BROIGNE D’ÉCAILLES French • Armor • Torso/Legs/Arms Posture Défensive: Once per turn, after you make a defense roll, you may have the roll gain +3. abilities.name: Placat
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1.

abilities.name: Apãrare glisantã
abilities.text: When you are attacked by a rival, you may decrease the value of one die in the attack roll by 1. Your next attack roll against that rival gains +1.

cards.flavor: Though providing excellent protection, plate armor was still vulnerable at the joints. Tassets protected the upper groin and hips of a warrior from frontal attack.

cards.name: BYRNIES

abilities.name: Stalwart
abilities.text: Add +2 to your defensive rolls for this round.

cards.flavor: Thousands of interlocking rings make this chain mail count worth its weight in protection.

cards.name: CAESAR’S GREAVES

abilities.name: Tutela Crusibus
abilities.text: After you roll doubles on a defense roll, gain 1 life.

cards.flavor: Shin guards protect warriors, especially mounted troops, from attacks against their legs.

cards.name: CAITHBHEART-CHUIM LEATHRACH

abilities.name: Gluasadachd
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you did not win initiative, you may immediately take one action. If you do, you get –1 speed this round.

cards.flavor: Covering a fighter’s torso, these light and flat pieces of armor were often covered in intricate designs that made each one a unique piece of art.

abilities.name: Basbaireadh Tuiteamach
abilities.text: Once per turn, after you are missed by an attack, you may immediately make a basic attack against an adjacent opponent at +0. You may use another Armor.

cards.flavor: Used to protect wrists and forearms during sword fights, vambraces were both simple to create and one of the most important pieces of armor to a Scottish warrior.

abilities.name: Îmbrãcare
abilities.text: Roll one die and place it on this card.

abilities.name: Fentã
abilities.text: Once each turn, you may swap one of the dice in an attack roll against you with the die on this card. At the end of each round, decrease the value of the die on this card by 1 if possible.

cards.flavor: Among the Dacians, only the aristocracy had the right to cover their heads.

cards.name: CASSIS

abilities.name: Galeatur
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +1 if you are a metal or water warrior. You gain +1 experience if you are an earth or fire warrior.

cards.flavor: Roman smiths crafted the cassis to be the most advanced, comfortable and functional helmet in the Roman Empire.

cards.name: CEANNASG MÀILLEACH

abilities.name: Air Fhaiceall ro Chunnart
abilities.text: After you are hit, if the defense roll plus two is greater than the attack roll, the attack deals –1 damage, to a minimum of 0.

cards.flavor: Covering head, neck, and shoulders, a mail coif could protect its wearer from disfigurement as well as death.

cards.name: CHAINMAIL SHIRT

abilities.name: Weardian de Ende
abilities.text: Your defense rolls gain +2 against sword and ranged weapons.

cards.flavor: Interlocking iron or steel rings easily turned aside the edge or point of any bladed weapon, Instead of slicing the skin, the wearer would often escape with little more than a bruise.

cards.name: CHIMALLI

abilities.name: Machtitenahuatiliztli
abilities.text: When you are attacked, if you are in the attacker’s basic attack grid, add the modifier from your space in the attacker’s basic attack grid to your defense roll.

cards.flavor: Aztec shields grew in beauty and extravagance with military rank. Of the eight surviving pieces of Aztec featherwork, five are chimalli.

cards.name: CLOGAID PHEMBRIDGE

abilities.name: Do-chrathte
abilities.text: Once per round, when you are attacked, replace your opponent’s modifier from their attack grid with any modifier from any of their attack grids.

cards.flavor: Named for Sir Richard Pembridge, these full-face helms provided excellent protection to the head and neck, though they limited both visibility and mobility.

cards.name: COPILLI

abilities.name: Ixcuepa
abilities.text: Twice each turn, if you have previously been hit this turn, you may re-roll your defense roll.

cards.flavor: This feather crown, made of gold and over 400 quetzal feathers, was a symbol of kingship that bore strong spiritual significance.

cards.name: COTA CORTA

abilities.name: Afortunado
abilities.text: When you are attacked with a weapon, if you have less experience than the attacker, your defense roll gains +1.

abilities.name: Malla Resistente
abilities.text: While you are in an opponent’s basic attack grid and he or she is not in your basic attack grid, your defense rolls gain +2.

cards.flavor: “El Cid Campeador” is derived from the Andalusi-Arabic term “al-sidi,” meaning “sir” or “lord” and the Spanish “el campeador”, meaning “army champion”.

cards.name: COTTE DE MAILLES DU PALADIN

abilities.name: Protection Renforcée
abilities.text: Before you are damaged by a critical hit, gain 3 life. When used correctly, chainmail could deflect otherwise mortal blows, allowing a professional soldier to survive the fury of battle. Its protection came, however, with a great deal of maintenance outside of battle.

cards.name: CUAUHCUACHIMALLI

abilities.name: Tlamatiliztli
abilities.text: You gain +2 experience.

abilities.name: Cuauhchicauhtiliztli
abilities.text: If you are dealt more than 2 damage from any one opponent in a round, you may attack twice with one of your weapons on your next turn.

cards.flavor: Worn by the Cuauhcuauhtin, or Eagle Warriors, these helmets denoted a warrior of great prowess - and high social and military rank – who had captured many enemies.

abilities.name: Seek Independence
abilities.text: Opponents cannot use abilities on cards in discard piles.

abilities.name: Retrial
abilities.text: Once each match, at the end of any round, if this card is in your discard pile, you may discard one of your face-up support cards to put this card into your warrior’s space.

cards.flavor: Born to a white man and a slave mother, James’ father appeared at court three times to acknowledge “the execution of a Deed of Emancipation from him to James, a mulatto boy.”

abilities.name: Yae
abilities.text: Once per turn, you may re-roll your defense roll.

cards.flavor: Eleven to thirteen overlapping plates make the do a very effective and flexible piece of body armor.

abilities.name: Seasamh Ildánach
abilities.text: If you lost initiative this round, you may re-roll one die of one of your attack rolls and one die of one of your defense rolls this round.

cards.flavor: “There is not in the world a warrior whom I shall not drive off.” – Cú Chulainn

abilities.name: Mantener Terreno
abilities.text: When you are attacked, the attacker cannot re-roll any die. Reduce the damage of all critical hits made against you to 1.

cards.flavor: Since 1438, and with the exception of Charles VII in 1742, the electors of the Holy Roman Empire always chose an emperor from the Hapsburg dynasty, to the extent that the title became akin to a family fiefdom.

cards.name: FEARSOME REPUTATION

abilities.name: Strike Fear
abilities.text: At the start of each round, each of your opponents rolls two dice. If the result is less than their life or if they are your rival, their rolls this round get –2.

cards.flavor: So terrifying was Blackbeard’s reputation that merchant ships were known to lose the will to fight the instant they recognized his flag.

abilities.name: Impulso Vital
abilities.text: Opponents may not use abilities on items or mystic cards.

cards.name: FORT BRIDGER

abilities.name: Barter

abilities.text: Opponents’ attack rolls against you cannot gain bonuses larger than +3.

cards.flavor: These ringed shirts provided excellent defense against slashing cuts and were also effective against thrusts, whilst remaining very flexible. As there were only interlinking rings to give it form, the armor suffered little from wear and could be repaired even when badly damaged.
cards.raw_source_text: (3) LORICA HAMATA Germanic • Armor • Torso Anpassungsfähig: Opponents’ attack rolls against you cannot gain bonuses larger than +3. These ringed shirts provided excellent defense against slashing cuts and were also effective against thrusts, whilst remaining very flexible. As there were only interlinking rings to give it form, the armor suffered little from wear and could be repaired even when badly damaged. Illustration: Randy Gallegos 3 49/100

cards.name: LORICAE SEGMENTATA

abilities.name: Mobilis
abilities.text: When you take more than 2 damage, reduce that damage to 2.

cards.flavor: This segmented armor, lightweight and flexible, revolutionizes chest protection.

abilities.name: Sraonadh
abilities.text: When you are attacked by a sword, axe, or knife, the attacker may choose to reduce their attack roll bonuses to 0. If they do not, the attack deals –1 damage to a minimum of 1.

cards.flavor: The Norse invaders of Ireland were so well equipped that only the Irish nobility could afford to arm and armor themselves in a similar fashion.

abilities.name: Stirixi
abilities.text: Before you are dealt damage from an attack, you may discard this card. If you do, that attack and the next attack against you deal –1 damage, to a minimum of 1.

cards.flavor: Given to Agamemnon as a gift, his cuirass had ten bands of dark enamel, twelve of gold, and twenty of tin. Dark blue serpents, three on either side, arched toward the neck.

abilities.name: Taitoatanga

Illustration: Miguel Coimbra 6 70/100

cards.name: BA DUAN JIN

abilities.name: Jun shi xun lian
abilities.text: If your inspiration and warrior are the same element, you can attack with your weapon a second time each round.

cards.flavor: The Eight-Section Brocade is an ideal lifetime exercise and was used by the troops of Yue Fie to condition themselves for battle.
cards.raw_source_text: (1) BA DUAN JIN Chinese • Special • Ability Jun shi xun lian: If your inspiration and warrior are the same element, you can attack with your weapon a second time each round. cards.name: BABIECA

abilities.name: Galope
abilities.text: Once each round, if you are a Wind warrior, after you are hit with an attack, you may move to any unoccupied space adjacent to the attacker.

cards.flavor: Pedro El Grande, El Cid’s godfather, gifted El Cid with his choice of horse. Upon choosing what seemed a weak and unsuitable stallion, Pedro exclaimed “Babieca!” meaning “Stupid!”
cards.raw_source_text: (2) BABIECA Spanish • Special • Cavalry Galope: Once each round, if you are a Wind warrior, after you are hit with an attack, you may move to any unoccupied space adjacent to the attacker. Upon choosing what seemed a weak and unsuitable stallion, Pedro exclaimed “Babieca!” meaning “Stupid!” Illustration: Hugh Jamieson 5 85/100

cards.name: BAD IM DRACHENBLUT

abilities.name: Unverwundbar
abilities.text: Attacks against you deal –1 damage, to a minimum of 0, for each die in the attack roll that is a 6.

cards.flavor: Subject of many songs and tales, Siegfried’s legend grew with every telling from the warrior-hero that he was to a nearinvulnerable dragon-slayer who was able to hide in plain view, and who wielded the sword of Odin himself.
cards.raw_source_text: (6) BAD IM DRACHENBLUT Germanic • Special • Item Unverwundbar: Attacks against you deal –1 damage, to a minimum of 0, for each die in the attack roll that is a 6. cards.name: BALLESTERO VASCO

abilities.name: Desplegar
abilities.text: Place this card in any space in the arena. If an opposing warrior ever moves into this card’s space, discard this card.

abilities.name: Disparar
abilities.text: Once each round, make a basic attack at +2 against any opposing warrior not adjacent to this card’s space.

cards.flavor: The deployment of Basque crossbowmen proved decisive in many of Charles V’s battles against the French and Italians.

cards.name: BALYEMEZ

abilities.name: Atma
abilities.text: You may discard a location card or support card in a space in the arena. If you have a rival, you may also discard any one of that player’s support cards.

cards.flavor: The Ottomans always demonstrated a great fondness for cannon. Generally limited to use in sieges, they were usually forged in situ, and abandoned afterward.
cards.raw_source_text: (4) BALYEMEZ Turkish � Special � Ranged Atma – Reveal: You may discard a location card or support card in a space in the arena. cards.name: BAND OF MERRIE MEN

abilities.name: Freonden Oder
abilities.text: Before initiative is determined each round, roll one die. If the result is less than 4, add that amount to your first attack roll of the round. If the result is 4 or greater, add that amount to your initiative for the round.

cards.flavor: Brought together by common cause, groups of outlaws and rogues were a common occurence in England during the Middle Ages.
cards.raw_source_text: (3) BAND OF MERRIE MEN Briton • Special • Ally Freonden Oder: Before initiative is determined each round, roll one die. Brought together by common cause, groups of outlaws and rogues were a common occurence in England during the Middle Ages. Illustration: Jeremy Jarvis 2 25/100

cards.name: BÁS SHEASAMHACH

abilities.name: Comhartha Misneach
abilities.text: If you lost the previous game, gain 2 life and +3 experience. Otherwise, gain 1 life and +1 experience. “… and he went to a pillar-stone that is in the plains, and he put his breast-girdle round it that he might not die seated nor lying down, but that he might die standing up.

cards.flavor: Then arrived the foe all around him, but they dared not go to him, for they thought he was alive.” – Annals of Ulster

cards.name: BELT OF HIPPOLYTE

abilities.name: Zoni Dexiotexnias
abilities.text: You gain +4 Experience.

cards.flavor: During Herakles’ ninth labor, he is forced to kill Amazonian Queen Hippolyte to take her belt back to Eurytheus.

cards.name: BERSERKERGANGR

abilities.name: Vrede
abilities.text: Your attacks deal +1 damage. You take 1 damage after each of your attacks.

cards.flavor: In fits of rage, beserkers and ulfheonars tear through enemy lines with inhuman strength and ferocity.

cards.name: BIAO QI JIANGJUN

abilities.name: Tong da
abilities.text: If you take more than one damage from basic attacks from the same warrior in a single turn, that warrior is your rival.

abilities.name: Zhong ji
abilities.text: Once each round, make a basic attack, if possible. For each cavalry card you have in play, the attack roll gets –1 and deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: In 221 AD, Ma Chao was appointed as Biao Qi Jiangjun (the General of the Agile Cavalry), governor of the Province of Liang and Marquis of Li Xiang.

cards.name: BING FA

abilities.name: Gong qi suo bu shou ye
abilities.text: Once per attack, before your attack roll, you may choose to reduce the roll by 3. If you do, that attack deals +1 damage.

cards.flavor: “The art of war is of vital importance to the State. It is a matter of life and death, a road either to safety or to ruin. Hence it is a subject of inquiry which can on no account be neglected. -Sun Tzu, The Art of War

abilities.name: Moit na Rèisimeid
abilities.text: Whenever you would roll two dice this round, roll three dice instead and choose which two to use.

cards.flavor: The three-feathered bonnet, along with the white rose of York, was the emblem of the Jacobite sympathizers.

abilities.name: Conairt Cogaidh
abilities.text: Place this card in any unoccupied space in the arena.

abilities.name: Ciap
abilities.text: At the end of each round, you may make one basic attack at +2 against each opposing warrior in the same square as, or adjacent to this card. This attack deals no more than 1 damage.

cards.flavor: Bran and Sceolang were Finn’s most faithful companions – intelligent, fierce and loyal.

cards.name: BUCEPHALUS

abilities.name: Strategike Thesis
abilities.text: Once per round, after you make an attack, you may move one space.

cards.flavor: "Ox Head", Alexander the Great’s faithful horse, carries him proudly into many battles.

cards.name: CARPAT

abilities.name: Si�e
abilities.text: Once per round, if you are adjacent to an opposing warrior and there is an empty space on the opposite side of them, move to that space and deal 2 damage to them. With its scythed wheels and light, fast build, the Carpat was capable of causing great damage to infantry.

cards.flavor: It was Boudicca’s greatest weapon against the Roman soldiers.

cards.name: CASSANDRA

abilities.name: Katara tis Dispistias
abilities.text: Before you are attacked, the attacker may choose to reduce all bonuses to their attack roll to 0. If they do not, and the attack hits, deal 1 damage to that opponent.

cards.flavor: Apollo fell in love with Cassandra, and granted her the gift of prophecy. When she did not return his feelings, he flew into a rage and cursed her, so that nobody would believe her visions.

cards.name: CETATEA POENARI

abilities.name: Patria strãmo’ilor
abilities.text: All of your rolls gain +1.

abilities.name: Asediat
abilities.text: At the start of each round, any opponent may deal 2 damage to their warrior. If they do, discard this card.

cards.flavor: Vlad’s wife hurled herself from the tower into the Arges River during a Turkish siege in 1462. Today, this section of the Arges is called Riul Doamnei, “The Lady’s River.”

cards.name: CHI TU MA

abilities.name: Chun zhui zhi zhi zhua huo
abilities.text: At the end of each round, if you have a rival, or if your initiative for this round is even, you may move one space. Then you may immediately attack a rival, if possible. For this attack, the defense roll cannot be modified.

cards.flavor: The mighty steed Chi Tu Ma, “Red Hare,” was part of a payment by Dong Zhuo to secure Lü Bu’s betrayal and murder of Ding Yuan, Lü Bu’s first master.

cards.name: CHIVALRY

abilities.name: A Cnihte Honur
abilities.text: Each round, if you win initiative, you may choose to go second. If you do, your attack and defense rolls this round gain +1.

cards.flavor: Thou shalt respect all weaknesses, and shalt constitute thyself the defender of them.

cards.name: CLÁIRSEACH BHORÚ

abilities.name: Comhcheol
abilities.text: If all of your face-up cards are Irish, your rolls gain +1.

cards.flavor: Although this harp was crafted in the fifteenth century, long after Ború died, the association between the two has been reinforced by hundreds of years of tradition. It is the national symbol of Ireland.

abilities.name: An Stáitse a Réiteach
abilities.text: Place this card in any space in the arena.

abilities.name: Buntáiste Bhaile
abilities.text: The first defense roll made each turn by opposing warriors in or adjacent to this card’s space get –2.

cards.flavor: On the beaches of Clontarf on Good Friday, April 23rd, 1014, Norse power in Ireland was shattered completely and forever in one of the bloodiest battles ever fought on Irish soil

cards.name: COURONNE IMPÉRIALE

abilities.name: Affirmation d’Autorité
abilities.text: Once per round, roll two dice. If the total is greater than your initiative for this round, gain 1 life. If the total is less than your experience, turn one of your support cards face up. At the end of the round, turn that card face down. If that card is discarded, use its initiative value as normal.

cards.flavor: “It is necessary that every man should seek to the best of his strength and ability to serve God and walk in the way of His precepts; for the Lord Emperor cannot watch over every man in personal discipline.” -Article of Law

cards.name: CRAOBH NAN CLACH

abilities.name: Binn Ciontaich
abilities.text: At the start of each round, if you win initiative, you may rotate an Aether, Fire, or Metal warrior.

cards.flavor: Many an interloper was hanged on the Tree of the Stones: “… To see the good fruit that grows on the Tree of Stones. Long, speckled fruit it is, wind-swayed by its yellow roots, And like men they are with their feet dancing in the void air.”

cards.name: CROWN OF ENGLAND

abilities.name: Konge
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 if you are a metal warrior. Your defense rolls get –1 and your attacks deal +1 damage if you are a fire warrior.

cards.flavor: The king wears this crown as a symbol of power and dominance over the English isles.

abilities.name: Tagair Ùghdaras
abilities.text: If you have no Armor in play, reduce all bonuses to your opponent’s rolls this round to 0. You may use another head card.

cards.flavor: Won through battle and murder, the crown of Scotland represented much more than kingship: it represented freedom gained at terrible cost, the defiance of tyranny, and the reclamation of a nation.

abilities.name: Motemachia
abilities.text: Each time you reveal a card (including this one), put a counter on this card. At the start of each round, you may remove a counter from this card. If you do, your rolls that round gain +1 for each counter on this card.

cards.flavor: This stone calendar, carved during the reign of Axayacotl, weighs about 25 tons and has a diameter of just less than 12 feet.

cards.name: CÙMHNANT BRISTE

abilities.name: Brathadh
abilities.text: At the start of any round, you may discard this card. If you do, all opponents’ rolls this round get –1 and you may discard an opponent’s item.

cards.flavor: Calum was determined to extend his kingdom southwards and take advantage of the upheaval caused by the Norman conquest of England. Making the excuse that he was supporting the claim to the English throne of his brother-in-law Edgar Atheling, Calum broke his treaties with England and invaded five times.

abilities.name: Gallia est omnis divisa in partes tres
abilities.text: Your attack rolls gain +1 while the total initiative of all of your cards is greater than the total initiative of any other warrior’s cards.

cards.flavor: Caesar’s scrolls outline his successes and experiences during the nine-year long Gallic campaigns.

abilities.name: Si vis pacem, para bellum
abilities.text: After you make an attack roll, you may increase the value of one of the dice by 1 if you won initiative this turn. If the dice are now doubles, the attack may result in a critical hit.

cards.flavor: The exceptional discipline of the Roman troops ensures their success across hundreds of battlefields.

cards.flavor: Saint Boniface had Thor’s Oak felled to prove the superiority of the Christian god. When Thor did not strike him down, the assembled locals agreed to be baptized.
